question: Check communication source code deeply for a hidden Ascend/MindSpeed load-balancing mechanism that prevents busy experts and congested cross-card communication.
conclusion: The visible Ascend/MindSpeed/TorchTitan-NPU communication code is count-aware and layout-aware, but it does not expose a hidden post-router expert reassignment scheduler. Load balance is router-side via expert_bias or debug force-balance; performance comes from count-aware all-to-all, local expert-major permute/padding, compact grouped matmul backward, and overlap scheduling.

ascend_router.read: expert_bias affects expert choice; debug moe_force_load_balance overrides selected expert ids.
num_tokens_per_expert is computed after routing and drives downstream communication/layout.

ascend_comm_layout.read: exchanges num_tokens_per_expert and derives all-to-all input/output splits.
after all-to-all, performs a local shuffle from rank-major to expert-major and pads local expert groups.
unpermute autograd saves sorted_indices/restore_shape and calls the NPU grad scatter operator.

ascend_grouped_math.read: torch_npu.npu_grouped_matmul is used for grouped forward, grad_input, and grad_weight.
expert forward is W13 grouped matmul, npu_swiglu, optional routed-score scale, W2 grouped matmul.

upstream_mindspeed_wrapper.read: wrapper computes topk/flatten size, allocates sorted_indices/permuted_tokens, and calls aclnnMoeTokenPermute.
backward calls aclnnMoeTokenPermuteGrad from saved sorted_indices/topk; no visible reassignment scheduler.
documented equivalent forward is argsort(flatten_indices) then index_select(tokens, sorted_indices // topk).
documented equivalent unpermute is index_copy, optional probability scaling, then sum across topk.

mori_transport.read: maxTotalRecvTokens is clamped/reported as capacity; not a load-balancing reassignment.
buffers are sized from worst-case/configured token counts; affects memory/transport capacity.
local expert counts are observed by scanning received ids and atomicAdd; no smoothing.

amd_current_dispatch_and_owner_vjp.read: standard dispatcher has count exchange, all-to-all, and expert-major local permute.
TorchAO variant adds expert-group padding for quantized grouped GEMMs.
active MORI/AITER path builds or reuses local route metadata by sorting local owner expert ids.
active owner VJP gathers compact rows, recomputes forward intermediates, and computes dTopK, dgrad/SwiGLU, wgrad, and xgrad through adapter-heavy pieces.

route_order_evidence.read: all_ranks_grouped_by_local_expert=false; random route has hundreds of runs per nonempty expert.
all_ranks_grouped_by_local_expert=false even under balanced routing; runs_per_nonempty_expert=384.0.

overlap_comparator.read: Primus split-BW path handles MoE overlap with separate wgrad scheduling.
Primus intercepts delayed TE wgrad closures for pipeline/zero-bubble scheduling.
Megatron grouped MLP exposes backward_dw for delayed/fused wgrad execution.
overlap schedule expands chunk order into layer-wise nodes and optional wgrad nodes.

mechanism_split: local expert-major token permute and padding
optional overlap of dX return/combine with delayed wgrad
amd_next_rung: make local owner rows expert-contiguous in an explicit compact layout
replace adapter-heavy owner VJP with fused compact grouped training backward for dW13, dW2, dX, and dTopKWeight
add dX-return and delayed-wgrad overlap after owner VJP tail is shortened
